messages.body: Why this condition Case WHEN [Issue ID] is null THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, Does not yield true or false?

### *********************************************************************
###
### Use this file to issue REST requests to the service
### using VS Code RFEST Client extension:
### https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=humao.rest-client
###
### *********************************************************************


### *****************************
### Obtain login for user admin

# @name login
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/auth/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token
Content-Type:  application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=password
&client_id=fabric-ui-root
&username=admin
&password=Gr33nD0t!

### *****************************
### List DDX Fields for an portfolio
GET https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/portfolios/32D0F153-250C-EE11-9155-005056A60C25/fields:ddx?filter=purpose="FILTER_EXPRESSION"
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

### *****************************
### List DDX Fields globally - Sandbox scenario
GET https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/fields:ddx?filter=purpose="FILTER_EXPRESSION"
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}


### *****************************
### List DDX Functions for an portfolio
GET https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/portfolios/C75D77E7-DB2A-ED11-9149-005056A60C25/functions:ddx
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}


### *****************************
### List DDX Functions globally - Sandbox scenario
GET https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/functions:ddx
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}


### *****************************
### Hydrate Expression globally - Sandbox scenario with a purpose and eval type
POST https://host.docker.internal:8081/v1/o/88fc8/modelExpressions:ddx?purpose=FILTER_EXPRESSION&ddx_evaluation_type=bool
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
    "friendly_description": "Case WHEN [Issue ID] is null THEN 1 ELSE 0 END"
}


### *****************************
### Hydrate Expression globally - Sandbox scenario with a purpose and eval type
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressions:ddx?purpose=FIELD_FORMULA&ddx_evaluation_type=value
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
    "friendly_description": "LesserOf2(AVG([Strata Number]), ABS([Strata Balance])) + ABS([Strata Balance])"
}

### *****************************
### Hydrate Expression globally - Sandbox scenario with a purpose and eval type, this time with ddx statement.
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressions:ddx?purpose=FILTER_EXPRESSION&ddx_evaluation_type=bool
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
    "ddx": "([lien_type] NOT IN ('AN') AND [lien_type] IS NOT NULL) OR FabricWithParams({'USA', 'BRA'}, '01/07/2024', '01/09/2024')"
}

### *****************************
### Hydrate Expression globally - Sandbox scenario with a purpose and eval type, this time with ddx statement.
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressions:ddx?purpose=FILTER_EXPRESSION&ddx_evaluation_type=bool
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
    "friendly_description": "[Lien Type] IS NOT ONE OF ('AN') or FabricWithParams({'USA', 'BRA'}, '01/07/2024', '01/09/2024')"
}

### *****************************
### Hydrate Expression globally - Sandbox scenario with eval type, this time with logic lines. Purpose is defaulted
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressions:ddx?ddx_evaluation_type=bool&purpose=ALL
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
  "logic": [
    {
      "logical_operator": null,
      "open_parenthesis": 0,
      "close_parenthesis": 0,
      "comparison_operator": "is not null",
      "comparison_value": null,
      "comparison_value2": null,
      "field_name": "strata_balance",
      "display_name": "Strata Balance",
      "friendly_description": "[Strata Balance] is not null"
    },
    {
      "logical_operator": "AND NOT",
      "open_parenthesis": 1,
      "close_parenthesis": 0,
      "comparison_operator": ">=",
      "comparison_value": "'1000'",
      "comparison_value2": null,
      "field_name": "stratum_id",
      "display_name": "Strata Number",
      "friendly_description": "[Strata Number] >= '1000'"
    },
    {
      "logical_operator": "OR NOT",
      "open_parenthesis": 0,
      "close_parenthesis": 1,
      "comparison_operator": "not like",
      "comparison_value": "'Tes%'",
      "comparison_value2": null,
      "field_name": "ca_description",
      "display_name": "Credit Agreement Description",
      "friendly_description": "[Credit Agreement Description] not like 'Moody's%'"
    }
  ]
}

### *****************************
### Hydrate Rules
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressionRules:ddx
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
  "friendly_description": "[Lien Type] is one of (<% Lien Type, LIEN_TYPE, TEXT, TRUE, V %>)"
}


### *****************************
### Hydrate Rules more complex
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressionRules:ddx
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
  "friendly_description": "dbo.RatingRankByType(<% Moody's Rating Field 2, MOODYS_ST_RATING, , FALSE, F %>, 'MOODYS_ST_RATING') - dbo.RatingRankByType(<% Moody's Rating Field 1, MOODYS_ST_RATING, , FALSE, F %>, 'MOODYS_ST_RATING') <% Comparison Operator, COMPARISON_OPERATOR, , FALSE, V %> <% Number of Subcategories, , NUMERIC, FALSE, V %>"
}

### *****************************
### Hydrate Rules more complex rule
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressionRules:ddx
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
  "friendly_description": "[Country] is one of (<% Country, COUNTRY_CODE, TEXT, True, F %>) and [First Payment Date] IS BETWEEN <% Begin First Payment Date, , DATE, FALSE, V %> and <% End First Payment Date, , DATE, FALSE, V %>"
}


### *****************************
### Hydrate Rules within rules
POST https://host.docker.internal:30081/compliance-modeling/v1/o/8af71/modelExpressionRules:ddx
content-type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{login.response.body.access_token}}

{
  "friendly_description": "FabricSubSubRule('Loan', {'Albania', 'American Samoa', 'China (People''s Republic of)'}, '01/01/2024', '12/01/2024')"
}

messages.body:
The expression `CASE WHEN [Issue ID] IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END` doesn't yield true/false because it returns an **integer** (1 or 0), not a **boolean**.

Looking at your request on line 56, you're sending it to the endpoint with `ddx_evaluation_type=bool`, which expects the expression to evaluate to a boolean result. But a `CASE...THEN 1 ELSE 0 END` expression evaluates to a **numeric value**, not a boolean.

To fix this, you have two options:

1. **Make the expression return a boolean directly** — just use the null check itself:
   ```
   [Issue ID] IS NULL
   ```
   or
   ```
   [Issue ID] IS NOT NULL
   ```

2. **Wrap it in a boolean comparison** — compare the CASE result to a value:
   ```
   CASE WHEN [Issue ID] IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END = 1
   ```

The core issue is that `CASE WHEN ... THEN 1 ELSE 0` is a value expression (returns a number), not a predicate (returns true/false). The DDX engine with `ddx_evaluation_type=bool` expects a predicate. Option 1 is the cleaner approach.

messages.body:
The errors confirm two separate issues:

1. **`CASE` is not a recognized keyword in your DDX grammar** — the parser doesn't understand `Case` at all (`"no viable alternative at input 'Case'"`). This DDX language simply doesn't support `CASE WHEN` syntax.

2. **The expression doesn't yield boolean** — as we discussed, even if it did parse, it wouldn't return true/false.

Since `CASE WHEN` isn't supported by the DDX parser, replace the expression with a direct boolean check. Change your `friendly_description` from:

```
Case WHEN [Issue ID] is null THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
```

to simply:

```
[Issue ID] IS NULL
```

This is natively supported by the DDX grammar (you can see similar `IS NOT NULL` patterns working in your other test cases in the same file, e.g., line 100), and it directly evaluates to a boolean — which is what `ddx_evaluation_type=bool` expects.

messages.body:
The parse tree tells the story. Looking at the root node:

- `parseTree.Payload` has `RuleIndex: 0` and `IsEmpty: true` — this is the **top-level start rule**, and it's **empty** (no valid boolean parse).
- Its first child (`GetChild(0)`) is an `ExpressionContext` (RuleIndex: 4), which successfully parsed the `CASE WHEN ... END` as a **value expression**.

The problem is that your DDX grammar has **two separate rule paths**:
- A **boolean/predicate** path (what `ddx_evaluation_type=bool` expects at the root)
- A **value/expression** path (`ExpressionContext`)

The `CASE WHEN [Issue ID] IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END` is being parsed as an `ExpressionContext` (a value expression returning a number), **not** as a boolean predicate. The root boolean rule (`RuleIndex: 0`) is `IsEmpty: true` because the parser couldn't match the CASE expression as a boolean — it fell through to the expression rule instead.

In short: your DDX grammar **does** recognize `CASE` syntax (it parsed successfully into `ExpressionContext`), but it classifies it as a **value expression**, not a **boolean expression**. The validation layer then rejects it because `ddx_evaluation_type=bool` requires the root to be a boolean predicate, not a value expression.

Your options:

1. **Use a direct boolean predicate:**
   ```
   [Issue ID] IS NULL
   ```

2. **Wrap the CASE in a boolean comparison** (if the grammar supports it):
   ```
   CASE WHEN [Issue ID] IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END = 1
   ```

3. **Modify the DDX grammar** to allow `CASE` expressions that return 1/0 to be implicitly treated as boolean predicates (this would require changes in the `DdxParser` grammar file to add CASE as an alternative in the boolean rule).

Option 1 is the simplest and most correct approach.

messages.body:
Now I can see the grammar rules. The issue is clear:

Your input `CASE WHEN [Issue ID] IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END` matches `searched_case_expression` because it uses `CASE condition_switch+ ...` (the `WHEN ... IS NULL` is a condition). The `condition_switch` likely resolves to `WHEN <boolean_condition> THEN <expression>`, and the `THEN 1 ELSE 0` part makes the overall CASE return a **value expression** (a number).

The `case_expression` rule itself is nested under the **expression** rule tree, not under the **boolean/predicate** rule tree. So regardless of whether the WHEN clause contains a boolean condition, the CASE as a whole evaluates to whatever the THEN/ELSE branches return — in this case, integers `1` and `0`.

To make it evaluate to boolean, you'd need to either:

1. **Use it directly as a boolean** (simplest):
   ```
   [Issue ID] IS NULL
   ```

2. **Make the THEN/ELSE return booleans** — but most SQL-like grammars (including yours) don't treat `CASE` as a predicate regardless of what the branches return.

3. **Modify the grammar** — add `case_expression` as an alternative in your boolean/predicate rule. Something like:

   ```antlr
   booleanExpression
     : ...existing alternatives...
     | case_expression    // allow CASE to be treated as boolean
     ;
   ```

   Or more precisely, add a `boolean_case_expression` variant where the THEN/ELSE branches must return boolean values:

   ```antlr
   boolean_case_expression
     : CASE condition_switch_bool+ else_bool_expression? END
     ;

   condition_switch_bool
     : WHEN booleanExpression THEN booleanExpression
     ;

   else_bool_expression
     : ELSE booleanExpression
     ;
   ```

The fundamental issue is architectural: in your ANTLR grammar, `case_expression` lives under the `expression` (value) rule, so the parser will never recognize it as a boolean predicate at the root level. Option 1 is the practical fix; option 3 is the grammar-level fix if you need CASE to work as a boolean.

messages.body: Now let me look at the grammar file and the related context classes to understand the CASE expression structure better.

content.content:
// Partially based on https://github.com/antlr/grammars-v4/blob/master/tsql/tsql.g4
grammar Ddx;
import DdxLexerRules;

@parser::members { public string dialect = "ddx"; }
@lexer::members { public string dialect = "ddx"; }

parse
 : condition EOF
 | expression EOF
 ;

condition
  : predicate
  | SYM_LP condition SYM_RP
  | function_call
  | NOT condition
  | condition AND condition
  | condition OR condition
  ;

predicate
  : {dialect == "ddx"}? expression PASS_THROUGH_CODE expression                 #DdxPredicatePassThroughCode
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression equals expression                            #DdxPredicateEquals
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_not_equal_to expression                   #DdxPredicateIsNotEqualTo
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_greater_than expression                   #DdxPredicateIsGreaterThan
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_less_than expression                      #DdxPredicateIsLessThan
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_greater_than_or_equal_to expression       #DdxPredicateIsGreaterThanOrEqual
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_less_than_or_equal_to expression          #DdxPredicateIsLessThanOrEqual
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_like expression                           #DdxPredicateIsLike
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_not_like expression                       #DdxPredicateIsNotLike
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_between expression AND expression         #DdxPredicateIsBetween
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_not_between expression AND expression     #DdxPredicateIsNotBetween
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_one_of SYM_LP expression_list SYM_RP      #DdxPredicateIsOneOf
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? expression is_not_one_of SYM_LP expression_list SYM_RP  #DdxPredicateIsNotOneOf
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression PASS_THROUGH_CODE expression                                                                                                                #SqlPredicatePassThroughCode
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression eq expression                                                                                                                               #SqlPredicateEq
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression eq expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                      #SqlPredicateEq
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression eq expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                           #SqlPredicateEq
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression neq expression                                                                                                                              #SqlPredicateNeq
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression neq expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                     #SqlPredicateNeq
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression neq expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                          #SqlPredicateNeq
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression gt expression                                                                                                                               #SqlPredicateGt
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression gt expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                      #SqlPredicateGt
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression gt expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                           #SqlPredicateGt
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression lt expression                                                                                                                               #SqlPredicateLt
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression lt expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                      #SqlPredicateLt
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression lt expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                           #SqlPredicateLt
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression gte expression                                                                                                                              #SqlPredicateGte
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression gte expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                     #SqlPredicateGte
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression gte expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                          #SqlPredicateGte
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression lte expression                                                                                                                              #SqlPredicateLte
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression lte expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                     #SqlPredicateLte
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression lte expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                          #SqlPredicateLte
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression like expression                                                                                                                             #SqlPredicateLike
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression like expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                    #SqlPredicateLike
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression like expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                         #SqlPredicateLike
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression not_like expression                                                                                                                         #SqlPredicateNotLike
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression not_like expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                                #SqlPredicateNotLike
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression not_like expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                     #SqlPredicateNotLike
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression between expression AND expression                                                                                                           #SqlPredicateBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression between expression AND expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                                  #SqlPredicateBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression between expression AND expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                       #SqlPredicateBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression between expression AND expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP            #SqlPredicateBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression not_between expression AND expression                                                                                                       #SqlPredicateNotBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression not_between expression AND expression AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                              #SqlPredicateNotBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression not_between expression AND expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                   #SqlPredicateNotBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression not_between expression AND expression AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP        #SqlPredicateNotBetween
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression in_stat SYM_LP expression_list SYM_RP                                                                                                       #SqlPredicateInStat
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression in_stat SYM_LP expression_list SYM_RP AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                              #SqlPredicateInStat
  | {dialect == "sql"}? expression not_in SYM_LP expression_list SYM_RP                                                                                                        #SqlPredicateNotIn
  | {dialect == "sql"}? SYM_LP expression not_in SYM_LP expression_list SYM_RP AND expression is_not_null SYM_RP                                                               #SqlPredicateNotIn
  | expression is_null #PredicateIsNull
  | expression is_not_null #PredicateIsNotNull
  ;

expression_list
    : expression (SYM_COMMA expression)*
    ;

expression
  : DEFAULT
  | NULL
  | {dialect == "ddx"}? field
  | full_column_name
  | literal
  | SYM_LP expression SYM_RP
  | SYM_TILDE expression
  | expression (SYM_ASTERISK | SYM_SLASH | SYM_PERCENT) expression
  | sign expression
  | function_call
  | set
  | odd_function_call
  | case_expression
  | pass_through_expression
  | select_statement
  | expression (SYM_PLUS |
                SYM_MINUS |
                SYM_AMPERSAND |
                SYM_CARET |
                SYM_PIPE) expression
  ;
/**
* Set
*/
set
  : SYM_LCB expression_list? SYM_RCB                           #SET
  ;

/**
* Function call expression
*/

function_call
  : function_name SYM_LP expression_list? SYM_RP                           #FC
  ;

function_name
  : (database_name=id SYM_DOT (schema_name=id)? SYM_DOT | (schema_name=id) SYM_DOT)? fn_name=id
  ;

id
  : ID
  | QUOTED_ID1
  | QUOTED_ID2
  // Include keywords like MAX, which can be seen as both an ID (MAX is a function call) as well as
  // a keyword (MAX is used in the in the data_type definition, e.g. NVARCHAR(MAX)).
  | keyword_as_id
  ;


/**
* The following rule covers some special SQL constructs which look 
* like functions, but include weird tokens which themselve don't look like parameters.
* For example, those would be CONVERT, CAST and IIF and all the functions which include a
* "datepart" parameter.
*/
odd_function_call
  : (CONVERT | TRY_CONVERT) SYM_LP data_type SYM_COMMA expression (SYM_COMMA convert_style)? SYM_RP
  | (CAST | TRY_CAST) SYM_LP expression AS data_type SYM_RP
  | (PARSE | TRY_PARSE) SYM_LP expression AS data_type (USING expression)? SYM_RP
  | IIF SYM_LP condition SYM_COMMA expression SYM_COMMA expression SYM_RP
  | (DATEADD | DATEDIFF | DATEDIFF_BIG) SYM_LP datepart SYM_COMMA expression SYM_COMMA expression SYM_RP
  | (DATENAME | DATEPART) SYM_LP datepart SYM_COMMA expression SYM_RP
  ;

convert_style
  : INT
  ;

data_type
  : id IDENTITY? data_type_length?
  ;

data_type_length
  : SYM_LP (first_int=INT | MAX) (SYM_COMMA second_int=INT)? SYM_RP
  ;

/**
* CASE expression
*/

/* https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms181765.aspx */
case_expression
  : simple_case_expression
  | searched_case_expression
  ;

simple_case_expression
  : CASE expression scalar_switch+ else_expression? END
  ;

searched_case_expression
  : CASE condition_switch+ else_expression? END
  ;

scalar_switch
  : WHEN when_expression=expression THEN result_expression=expression
  ;

condition_switch
  : WHEN condition THEN result_expression=expression
  ;

else_expression
  : ELSE expression ;


/**
* Pass-through expression
*/

pass_through_expression
  : PASS_THROUGH_CODE
  ;

/**
* SELECT expression
*/

select_statement
  : query_expression order_by_clause?
  ;

query_expression
  : (query_specification | SYM_LP query_expression SYM_RP) union*
  ;

union
  : (UNION ALL? | EXCEPT | INTERSECT) (query_specification | (SYM_LP query_expression SYM_RP)+)
  ;

query_specification
  : SELECT (ALL | DISTINCT)? (TOP expression PERCENT? (WITH TIES)?)?
    select_list
    // https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms188029.aspx
    (FROM table_sources)?
    (WHERE where=search_condition)?
    // https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms177673.aspx
    (GROUP BY group_by_item (SYM_COMMA group_by_item)*)?
    (HAVING having=search_condition)?
  ;

// https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms176104.aspx
select_list
  : select_list_elem (SYM_COMMA select_list_elem)*
  ;

select_list_elem
  : full_column_name
  | (table_name SYM_DOT)? (SYM_ASTERISK | SYM_DOLLAR (IDENTITY | ROWGUID))
  | column_alias SYM_EQ expression
  | expression (AS? column_alias)?
  | count_expression (AS? column_alias)?
  ;

count_expression
  : COUNT SYM_LP count_elem SYM_RP
  ;

count_elem
  : full_column_name
  | SYM_ASTERISK
  | column_alias
  | expression
  ;

column_alias_list
  : column_alias (SYM_COMMA column_alias)*
  ;

column_alias
  : id
  | STRING
  ;

table_sources
  : table_source (SYM_COMMA table_source)*
  ;

table_source
  : table_source_item_joined
  | SYM_LP table_source_item_joined SYM_RP
  ;

table_source_item_joined
  : table_source_item join_part*
  ;

table_source_item
  : table_name_with_hint as_table_alias?
  | rowset_function as_table_alias?
  | derived_table (as_table_alias (SYM_LP column_alias_list SYM_RP)?)?
  | change_table as_table_alias
  | function_call as_table_alias?
  ;

table_name
  : (database_name=id SYM_DOT (schema_name=id)? SYM_DOT | schema_name=id SYM_DOT)? tbl_name=id
  ;

full_column_name
    : (table_name SYM_DOT)? id
    ;

as_table_alias
  : AS? table_alias
  ;

table_alias
  : id with_table_hints?
  ;

table_name_with_hint
  : table_name with_table_hints?
  ;

// https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms187373.aspx
with_table_hints
  : WITH? SYM_LP table_hint (SYM_COMMA table_hint)* SYM_RP
  ;

// Id runtime check. Id can be (FORCESCAN, HOLDLOCK, NOLOCK, NOWAIT, PAGLOCK, READCOMMITTED,
// READCOMMITTEDLOCK, READPAST, READUNCOMMITTED, REPEATABLEREAD, ROWLOCK, TABLOCK, TABLOCKX
// UPDLOCK, XLOCK)
table_hint
  : NOEXPAND? ( INDEX SYM_LP index_value (SYM_COMMA index_value)* SYM_RP
  | INDEX SYM_EQ index_value
  | FORCESEEK (SYM_LP index_value SYM_LP ID  (SYM_COMMA ID)* SYM_RP SYM_RP)?
  | SERIALIZABLE
  | SNAPSHOT
  | SPATIAL_WINDOW_MAX_CELLS SYM_EQ INT
  | FORCESCAN
  | HOLDLOCK
  | NOLOCK
  | NOWAIT
  | PAGLOCK
  | READCOMMITTED
  | READCOMMITTEDLOCK
  | READPAST
  | READUNCOMMITTED
  | REPEATABLEREAD
  | ROWLOCK
  | TABLOCK
  | TABLOCKX)?
  ;

index_value
  : id | INT
  ;

// https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms190312.aspx
rowset_function
  :  (
	    OPENROWSET SYM_LP provider_name = STRING SYM_COMMA connectionString = STRING SYM_COMMA sql = STRING SYM_RP
	  )
	  | ( OPENROWSET SYM_LP BULK data_file=STRING SYM_COMMA (bulk_option (SYM_COMMA bulk_option)* | id)SYM_RP )
  ;

change_table
  : CHANGETABLE SYM_LP CHANGES table_name SYM_COMMA (NULL | INT ) SYM_RP
  ;

bulk_option
  : id SYM_EQ bulk_option_value = (INT | STRING)
  ;

derived_table
  : subquery
  | SYM_LP subquery SYM_RP
  ;

subquery
  : select_statement
  ;

// https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms188385.aspx
order_by_clause
  : ORDER BY order_by_expression (SYM_COMMA order_by_expression)*
    (OFFSET expression (ROW | ROWS) (FETCH (FIRST | NEXT) expression (ROW | ROWS) ONLY)?)?
  ;

order_by_expression
  : expression (ASC | DESC)?
  ;

group_by_item
  : expression
  ;

// https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms191472.aspx
join_part
  // https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms173815(v=sql.120).aspx
  : (INNER? |
      join_type=(LEFT | RIGHT | FULL) OUTER?) (join_hint=(LOOP | HASH | MERGE | REMOTE))?
      JOIN table_source ON search_condition
  | CROSS JOIN table_source
  | CROSS APPLY table_source
  | OUTER APPLY table_source
  ;


search_condition
  : condition
  ;

/**
* [FIELD] expression
*/

field: (table_name SYM_DOT)? FIELD;


/**
 * Alternative DDX predicate operators
 *
 * equals: '=' ;
 * is_not_equal_to: '<>' ;
 * is_greater_than: '>' ;
 * is_less_than: '<' ;
 * is_greater_than_or_equal_to: '>=' ;
 * is_less_than_or_equal_to: '<=' ;
 * is_like: LIKE ;
 * is_not_like: NOT LIKE ;
 * is_null: IS NULL ;
 * is_not_null: IS NOT NULL ;
 * is_one_of: IN ;
 * is_not_one_of: NOT IN ;
 * is_between: BETWEEN ;
 * is_not_between: NOT BETWEEN ;
*/

/* Version with friendly syntax */

equals: EQUALS ;
is_not_equal_to: IS NOT EQUAL TO ;
is_greater_than: IS GREATER THAN ;
is_less_than: IS LESS THAN ;
is_greater_than_or_equal_to: IS GREATER THAN OR EQUAL TO ;
is_less_than_or_equal_to: IS LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO ;
is_like: IS LIKE ;
is_not_like: IS NOT LIKE ;
is_null: IS NULL ;
is_not_null: IS NOT NULL ;
is_one_of: IS ONE OF ;
is_not_one_of: IS NOT ONE OF ;
is_between: IS BETWEEN ;
is_not_between: IS NOT BETWEEN ;


/**
 * SQL datepart
 *
 */
datepart
  : YEAR | YY | YYYY
  | QUARTER | QQ | Q_
  | MONTH | MM | M_
  | DAYOFYEAR | DY | Y_
  | DAY | DD | D_
  | WEEK | WK | WW
  | WEEKDAY | DW
  | HOUR | HH
  | MINUTE | MI | N_
  | SECOND | SS | S_
  | MILLISECOND | MS
  | MICROSECOND | MCS
  | NANOSECOND | NS
  | TZOFFSET | TZ
  | ISO_WEEK | ISOWK | ISOWW
  ;

/**
 * SQL Keywords, that can also be seen as IDs
 * The list includes keywords like MAX, which can be seen as both an ID (MAX is a function call) as well as
 * a keyword (MAX is used in the in the data_type definition, e.g. NVARCHAR(MAX)).
 *
 */
keyword_as_id
  : MAX
  | COUNT
  | YEAR
  | YY
  | YYYY
  | QUARTER
  | QQ
  | MONTH
  | MM
  | DAYOFYEAR
  | DY
  | DAY
  | DD
  | WEEK
  | WK
  | WW
  | WEEKDAY
  | DW
  | HOUR
  | HH
  | MINUTE
  | MI
  | SECOND
  | SS
  | MILLISECOND
  | MS
  | MICROSECOND
  | MCS
  | NANOSECOND
  | NS
  | TZOFFSET
  | TZ
  | ISO_WEEK
  | ISOWK
  | ISOWW 
  | Q_
  | M_
  | Y_
  | D_
  | N_
  | S_
  ;

/**
 * SQL predicate operators
 *
 */

eq: SYM_EQ ;
neq: lt gt | '<>' ; // Note that SQL allows for '<>' to be typed in as '< >' (with a space between the gt and eq).
             // This why we need to use parser rules instead of lexer rules.
gt: SYM_GT ;
lt: SYM_LT ;
gte: gt eq | '>=' ; // Note that SQL allows for '>=' to be typed in as '> =' (with a space between the gt and eq)
lte: lt eq | '<=' ; // Note that SQL allows for '>=' to be typed in as '> =' (with a space between the gt and eq)
like: LIKE ;
not_like: NOT LIKE ;
between: BETWEEN ;
not_between: NOT BETWEEN ;
in_stat: IN ;
not_in: NOT IN ;


literal
  : STRING
  | sign? INT
  | sign? (REAL | FLOAT)
  ;

sign
  : SYM_PLUS
  | SYM_MINUS
  ;
